zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Houghton於1342年出生在英國時，他的父親Adam de Hoghton33歲，母親Ellen Venables31歲。他於1375年與Joan De Radcliffe在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1422年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓享年80歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Houghton于1342年出生在英国时，他的父亲Adam de Hoghton33岁，母亲Ellen Venables31岁。他于1375年与Joan De Radcliffe在英国藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1422年在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯頓享年80岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Houghton was born in 1342, in United Kingdom, his father, Adam de Hoghton, was 33 and his mother, Ellen Venables, was 31. He married Joan De Radcliffe in 1375, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1422, in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 80, and was buried in Lancashire, England.

Il a épousé Joan De Radcliffe en 1375, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1422, à Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Lancashire, Angleterre.

Er hat 1375 in Lancashire, England Joan De Radcliffe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1422 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Houghton è nato il nel 1342 nel Regno Unito, il suo padre, Adam de Hoghton, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Ellen Venables, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Joan De Radcliffe nel 1375, nel Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1422, nel Preston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolto a Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghtonが1342年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Adam de Hoghtonは33歳，母Ellen Venablesは31歳であった。1375年にイギリスランカシュアイアでJoan De Radcliffeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1422年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンで80歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton(이)가 1342년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam de Hoghton(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen Venables(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1375년 영국 랭카실르에서 Joan De Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1422년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 플레스턴에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Houghton nasceu em 1342, em United Kingdom, seu pai, Adam de Hoghton, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Ellen Venables, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan De Radcliffe em 1375, em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1422, em Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Houghton родился в 1342 г. в местности Великобритания, его отцу по имени Adam de Hoghton было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Ellen Venables, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan De Radcliffe в 1375 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1422 г. в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности ланкашир, Англия.
